Convert Hugo Markdown posts into Nectar content

Usage:
  nectar import-hugo [--on-conflict <skip|overwrite|rename>] [--dry-run] [--json] <dir>

Arguments:
  dir                 Path to a Hugo project root. Nectar scans content/posts/, content/post/, content/blog/, then content/.

End of options:
  Use `--` before positional values that start with `-`, for example `nectar new post -- --config`.

Options:
  --on-conflict <skip|overwrite|rename>  How to handle existing files when slugs collide: skip (default), overwrite, or rename
  --dry-run           Scan Markdown and print a summary of what would land, including redirects from aliases, without writing files
  -j, --json          Emit the import summary as JSON on stdout for CI consumption
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .nectarrc > config > default).
  Naming: NECTAR_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --on-conflict → NECTAR_IMPORT_HUGO_ON_CONFLICT

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  nectar import-hugo ../old-hugo-site
  nectar import-hugo ../old-hugo-site --dry-run
  nectar import-hugo ../old-hugo-site --on-conflict rename
